articles.title: The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential
articles.summary: Major AI companies like OpenAI and Anthropic face a critical monetization cliff, struggling to become profitable businesses before running out of funding. The industry's race for profits has become an existential challenge for these leading firms.

articles.title: Google makes it easy to deepfake yourself
articles.summary: YouTube Shorts launches an AI feature allowing creators to easily clone themselves on camera for realistic deepfake videos. The rollout reflects the platform's complex approach to generative AI content.

articles.title: Gemini gets notebooks to help you organize projects
articles.summary: Google Gemini now features 'notebooks' to organize project-related information in one place, allowing users to incorporate files, conversations, and custom instructions as context during chats.

articles.title: Amazon CEO takes aim at Nvidia, Intel, Starlink, more in annual shareholder letter
articles.summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy defends the company's $200 billion capital expenditure by critiquing competitors including Nvidia, Intel, and Starlink in his annual shareholder letter.

articles.title: Final 2 days to save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket
articles.summary: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 tickets are discounted up to $500 until April 10 at 11:59 p.m. PT. After this deadline, prices will increase, so registering now locks in savings.

articles.title: Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
articles.summary: Poke simplifies AI agent access through text messaging, enabling users to automate tasks without technical expertise or complex setup.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with two million developers, raised $100 million Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code costs $20-$200 monthly for AI coding, sparking developer backlash. Goose, an open-source alternative by Block, offers identical features for free with no subscription.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge to recruit engineers. The creative hiring stunt decoded to a Berghain nightclub algorithm puzzle that attracted thousands of applicants.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iranian government-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers in factories, water treatment centers, and refineries. Six US agencies issued urgent warnings about the advanced persistent threat group's operations since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russian military's APT28 hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally, redirecting users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage operations targeting governments.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a popular AI agentic tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, poses security risks by requiring extensive system access. A recently fixed vulnerability highlights dangers of granting such broad permissions to autonomous AI agents.

articles.title: The Download: AstroTurf wars and exponential AI growth
articles.summary: Synthetic turf installation in the US surged from 7 million to 79 million square meters since 2001, raising concerns about microplastics pollution despite industry claims of safety.

articles.title: Is fake grass a bad idea? The AstroTurf wars are far from over.
articles.summary: Cornell University installed synthetic turf for field hockey, replacing a natural meadow. Environmental advocates like Yayoi Koizumi oppose the artificial field due to ecological concerns.

articles.title: Desalination technology, by the numbers
articles.summary: Desalination technology converts seawater into fresh water and is increasingly vital for water-stressed regions. Qatar produces 77% of its fresh water through desalination, highlighting the technology's critical importance globally.

articles.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers now supports multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling processing of text, images, and other data types together for improved semantic understanding and ranking tasks.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve continuously while performing tasks. The system allows agents to adapt and enhance their capabilities through on-the-job experience.

articles.title: Safetensors is Joining the PyTorch Foundation
articles.summary: Safetensors, a secure file format for storing machine learning models, is joining the PyTorch Foundation. This partnership aims to improve model safety and standardization across the ML community.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Microsoft's Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its potential applications.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to boost productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing. These AI solutions enable companies to work more efficiently while maintaining imaginative outputs.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released its open-source 'farm of the future' toolkit, enabling developers to build agricultural technology solutions using AI and cloud computing for modern farming practices.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces two new inference tiers for the Gemini API: Flex for cost-effective options and Priority for lower latency, helping developers balance expenses and performance.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI-powered video creation and editing capabilities using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling high-quality video generation at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect Brazil's forests.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google released Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence and performance.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live improves voice AI with enhanced precision and lower latency, enabling more fluid and natural voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches how AI systems can manipulate people in finance and health sectors, developing new safety measures to prevent harmful exploitation.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family while other significant AI developments were discussed.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, a generative AI technology that boosts video game photorealism in real-time up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate enhanced visual details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: Agentic AI’s governance challenges under the EU AI Act in 2026
articles.summary: AI agents risk creating governance challenges as they can act without clear audit trails, making compliance with the EU AI Act's August enforcement difficult. Organizations must implement proper controls and traceability to avoid substantial penalties for high-risk AI applications.

articles.title: Anthropic keeps new AI model private after it finds thousands of external vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview AI model discovered thousands of cybersecurity vulnerabilities across major operating systems and browsers. Rather than releasing it publicly, the company shared access with tech giants and critical infrastructure organizations through Project Glasswing.

articles.title: AI’s software development success and central management needs
articles.summary: AI adoption is rapidly entering production phases, but enterprises lack sufficient governance and integration controls. A survey of IT leaders reveals a critical gap between AI capabilities and organizational safety measures.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture addressing robot navigation challenges in complex indoor environments. It tackles localization, self-positioning, and path planning for general-purpose mobile robots.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering systems surrounding them are emerging as the true competitive advantage. The Anthropic Claude Code leak demonstrates that the harness engineering matters more than the model itself.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware targets OpenClaw AI agents running on Mac Minis, exploiting macOS binaries to steal credentials as developers deploy local AI agents.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by effectively balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming a major limitation in AI models.

articles.title: Sigmoid vs ReLU Activation Functions: The Inference Cost of Losing Geometric Context
articles.summary: Sigmoid activation functions compress inputs into a narrow range, losing geometric context and weakening neural network representations. ReLU preserves magnitude information, enabling deeper models to remain expressive with less computational overhead.

articles.title: A Coding Guide to Build Advanced Document Intelligence Pipelines with Google LangExtract, OpenAI Models, Structured Extraction, and Interactive Visualization
articles.summary: This tutorial demonstrates using Google's LangExtract library with OpenAI models to extract structured data from unstructured documents like contracts and meeting notes, then visualize results for analytics and automation.

articles.title: Google AI Research Introduces PaperOrchestra: A Multi-Agent Framework for Automated AI Research Paper Writing
articles.summary: Google AI Research introduces PaperOrchestra, a multi-agent framework that automatically converts unstructured research materials into submission-ready LaTeX manuscripts with literature reviews, figures, and verified citations.

articles.title: Why Ubuntu 26.04 will thrill gamers - and it's not just the performance bump
articles.summary: Ubuntu 26.04 offers gamers improved performance and new replacement applications. The release also removes unnecessary components, enhancing overall gaming experience.

articles.title: The best Android tablets of 2026: Lab tested, expert recommended
articles.summary: ZDNET tested top Android tablets from Samsung, TCL, and others as alternatives to iPads. The lab-tested recommendations showcase diverse options beyond Apple's tablet ecosystem.

articles.title: The best dedicated web hosting of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
articles.summary: Expert review of top dedicated web hosting providers in 2026, evaluating performance, security, and reliability.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Agentic AI Design Patterns
articles.summary: Article explores design patterns for building agentic AI systems. Provides guidance on mastering autonomous AI agents and their implementation strategies.

articles.title: A Hands-On Guide to Testing Agents with RAGAs and G-Eval
articles.summary: Guide on testing AI agents using RAGAs and G-Eval frameworks. Covers hands-on approaches for evaluating agent performance and reliability in production environments.

articles.title: Handling Race Conditions in Multi-Agent Orchestration
articles.summary: Race conditions occur when multiple agents simultaneously write to the same resource, producing corrupted or nonsensical results. The article addresses managing these conflicts in multi-agent systems.

articles.title: A Visual Explanation of Linear Regression
articles.summary: A comprehensive guide with 100+ visualizations explaining linear regression, covering model building, quality measurement, and improvement techniques.

articles.title: How Visual-Language-Action (VLA) Models Work
articles.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models integrate visual perception, language understanding, and action execution for humanoid robots, based on mathematical foundations that enable unified decision-making.

articles.title: A Survival Analysis Guide with Python: Using Time-To-Event Models to Forecast Customer Lifetime
articles.summary: Learn survival analysis techniques using Python to model customer retention through Kaplan-Meier curves and Cox Proportional Hazard regressions for forecasting customer lifetime value.

articles.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
articles.summary: Samson: A Tyndalston Story, a game about grit and faith, is now available on GeForce NOW for cloud streaming across nearly any device.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights breakthroughs in physical AI and robots transforming industries. Advancements in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate real-world deployment from virtual training.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA collaborated to optimize Gemma 4, a new family of small, fast models designed for efficient local AI execution across devices from RTX PCs to data centers, enabling on-device agentic AI.

articles.title: Introducing stateful MCP client capabilities on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime now supports stateful MCP client capabilities enabling interactive multi-turn agent workflows. Three new features—elicitation, sampling, and progress notification—allow agents to pause execution for user input, request LLM content, and stream real-time updates.

articles.title: Customize Amazon Nova models with Amazon Bedrock fine-tuning
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock now enables customization of Amazon Nova models through fine-tuning techniques like supervised fine-tuning, allowing businesses to tailor models with proprietary knowledge and industry-specific workflows.

articles.title: Human-in-the-loop constructs for agentic workflows in healthcare and life sciences
articles.summary: AI agents in healthcare require human-in-the-loop oversight to ensure regulatory compliance and patient safety. AWS provides practical approaches for implementing HITL constructs in sensitive medical operations.

articles.title: Cut Checkpoint Costs with About 30 Lines of Python and NVIDIA nvCOMP
articles.summary: NVIDIA's nvCOMP tool reduces LLM checkpoint storage costs using just 30 lines of Python. Checkpoints for large models like 70B parameters can exceed 782GB, making compression essential for training efficiency.

articles.title: How to Accelerate Protein Structure Prediction at Proteome-Scale
articles.summary: The article discusses accelerating protein structure prediction at proteome-scale, focusing on how proteins function through interactions forming complexes rather than as isolated molecules.

articles.title: Integrate Physical AI Capabilities into Existing Apps with NVIDIA Omniverse Libraries
articles.summary: NVIDIA's Omniverse Libraries enable developers to integrate Physical AI capabilities into existing applications, allowing teams to design and validate robots in simulated environments before deployment.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing it openly with others.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background leveraged unique perspectives to develop innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthrough advances in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and understanding of model behavior, advancing safer and more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents information-driven methods for designing imaging systems that optimize how well measurements distinguish objects. An encoder maps objects to images which noise then corrupts, with an information estimator quantifying measurement quality.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, avoiding TD scalability issues and handling long-horizon tasks effectively.
